doc.title: Debits and credits in action
doc.event: Maya's studio has three events this week: it buys a $1,200 computer with cash; it pays $800 rent; it bills a client $2,000 for finished work. Record each — and watch the rules, not your instincts.

variants.label: One asset trades for another — equation unchanged

lines.label: asset up → debit
lines.why: Equipment is an asset arriving — assets grow with debits.
lines.trap: Not an expense! The computer will serve for years — it's a resource acquired, not a cost used up. (Using it up over time is depreciation — Ch. 5's world.)

lines.label: asset down → credit
lines.why: Cash is an asset leaving — assets shrink with credits.
lines.trap: Total assets didn't change: one asset swapped for another. A = L + E never even flinched.

variants.label: A cost is used up — equity shrinks via an expense

lines.label: expense up → debit
lines.why: This month's space is used up as you occupy it — a cost consumed NOW is an expense, and expenses grow with debits.
lines.trap: 'Debit' does not mean bad and 'credit' does not mean good — they're directions, not judgments. Expenses are debit-normal because they REDUCE equity.

lines.label: asset down → credit
lines.trap: Paying NEXT month's rent in advance would be Prepaid Rent — an asset — not an expense. When the benefit arrives decides the account.

variants.label: Work is earned — equity grows via revenue (no cash yet!)

entries.caption: Bill a client $2,000 for completed work

lines.label: asset up → debit
lines.why: A right to collect $2,000 is an asset the moment the work is DONE.
lines.trap: Revenue doesn't wait for cash. Earned = revenue, even if the money comes next month. This one idea (accrual accounting) is half of Intro 1.

lines.label: revenue up → credit
lines.why: Revenue grows with credits because earnings grow EQUITY, and equity is credit-normal.
lines.trap: Crediting Cash here is the #1 beginner reflex — but no cash moved. The credit answers 'why did assets grow?': because we earned it.

memorize.body: The whole rulebook: Assets & Expenses grow with DEBITS. Liabilities, Equity & Revenues grow with CREDITS. Shrinking = the opposite side. Every entry: debits = credits.
Ask two questions per line: WHICH family is this account? and did it GROW or SHRINK? The rules do the rest — instincts about 'debit sounds negative' will only betray you.

questions.prompt: The studio bills a client $2,000 for finished work (payment due in 30 days). The entry is…

distractors.feedback: 'No cash, no entry' is the cash-basis trap. The work is EARNED, so revenue is recognized now: Dr Accounts Receivable 2,000 / Cr Service Revenue 2,000. Cash arriving later just swaps AR for Cash.

build.scaffold: Per line: name the family, name the direction, apply DEALER. Never start from which word 'sounds right.'
